commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8e2cbb95af0365f0c47708b64284704c3d54f402)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a finalAssistantVisibleText-based recovery gate in `resolveCronPayloadOutcome` and updates helper tests for recovered cron agentTurn error classification.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +49. Total +57 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still routes fatal structured-error classification into the pre-dispatch return path, and the canonical issue has a real reduced scheduled-cron repro. I did not run a live cron job in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-visible proof and a blocking patch correction before merge; ClawSweeper should not queue repair while real behavior proof is missing.

target.title: fix(cron): classify recovered agentTurn as non-fatal when finalAssistantVisibleText exists
target.body:
## Summary

Cron `agentTurn` runs that encounter a transient tool error mid-run and recover with a successful `finalAssistantVisibleText` are now classified as `ok` instead of `error`, ensuring the success announce delivery is dispatched.

- **Problem**: `resolveCronPayloadOutcome` classified runs as fatal when no existing recovery gate could fire. The four gates all failed because the raw exec error payload lacked the required metadata markers (`nonTerminalToolErrorWarning`), text prefixes (`⚠️ 🛠️`), or deliverable post-error payloads (`hasSuccessfulPayloadAfterLastError`). The agent's `finalAssistantVisibleText` — which proved recovery — was never consulted.
- **Solution**: Add `hasRecoveredByFinalAnswer` gate that treats a non-empty `finalAssistantVisibleText` as recovery proof when the session completed without a run-level error or fatal failure signal.
- **What changed**: `src/cron/isolated-agent/helpers.ts` (5 lines of gate logic + comment), `src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts` (2 updated tests + 3 new tests)
- **What did NOT change**: Run-level error handling, `fatalForCron` failure signal precedence, delivery dispatch logic, channel policy, SDK surface, or any config/migration paths.

## Change Type & Scope

### Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

### Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue

- Related to #96255
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A cron `agentTurn` with `delivery.mode: "announce"` was silently failing every night for 6 consecutive days. The agent hit a transient `exec` tool error (shell redirect syntax rejected), retried successfully, completed 60+ further tool calls, and emitted a full final report — but the operator received no report at all. The cron run log showed `status=error` with a synthesized failure notification built from the already-recovered tool error.

This affects any cron whose agent habitually probes with shell-isms the `exec` tool rejects (`>`, `2>&1`, `;`, quoted python one-liners) and then retries — a common recovery pattern across multiple model providers.

## Review Contract

```text
Ref: #96255 / PR #TBD
Surface: cron / isolated-agent outcome classification

Bug: Recovered tool errors misclassified as fatal when finalAssistantVisibleText proves recovery
Cause: hasFatalStructuredErrorPayload lacked a gate consulting finalAssistantVisibleText;
  all four existing gates require specific payload markers or deliverability that plain exec
  errors don't carry (src/cron/isolated-agent/helpers.ts:299-304)
  置信度: clear
Provenance:
  introduced by: hasRecoveredToolWarning gate (PR #TBD) — only covers ⚠️ 🛠️ prefixed errors
  made visible by: agent model behavior change — more frequent plain exec retry patterns
  置信度: likely

Best fix: Yes. Adding a finalAssistantVisibleText-based gate is the minimal correct fix
  because finalAssistantVisibleText is the agent runtime's canonical signal of completion.
  The alternative (broadening isSuccessfulCronPayload) would change downstream delivery
  selection behavior and doesn't cover cases where no post-error reply payloads exist.
Refactor: No — the existing gate structure already handles structured/content-specific
  recovery patterns; this adds the missing general case.
Proof: 单元测试 (31 tests pass, 3 new + 2 updated covering the fix)
Risk: If runLevelError is somehow unset for a genuinely unrecoverable provider error,
  a partial finalAssistantVisibleText could suppress an error report. The !runLevelError
  guard mitigates this — provider errors typically set runLevelError.
```

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed**: Cron `agentTurn` with recovered tool error + `finalAssistantVisibleText` → misclassified as `error`, delivery skipped.

**Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112, Node 22.19+, branch `fix/cron-recovered-final-answer-96255`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts --run
pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts --run
pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run.payload-fallbacks.test.ts --run
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  31 passed (31)

$ pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  51 passed (51)

$ pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run.payload-fallbacks.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
```

Recovery classification matrix:

```
Input scenario                                     => hasFatalErrorPayload
exec error (unmarked) + finalAssistantVisibleText   => false  (recovered)    ← #96255 fix
exec error + runLevelError + finalAssistantVisible  => true   (still fatal)  ← guard
exec error + fatalForCron signal + finalAnswer      => true   (still fatal)  ← guard
⚠️ 🛠️ tool warning + finalAssistantVisibleText      => false  (existing gate)
nonTerminalToolErrorWarning + finalAnswer           => false  (existing gate)
⚠️ ✉️ Message failed + finalAssistantVisibleText    => false  (existing gate)
error payload, no finalAssistantVisibleText         => true   (unchanged)
```

**Observed result after fix**:

- `hasFatalStructuredErrorPayload` is false when `finalAssistantVisibleText` proves recovery
- Existing recovery gates (`hasRecoveredToolWarning`, `hasNonTerminalToolErrorWarning`, `hasPendingPresentationWarning`) continue to work
- `runLevelError` and `fatalForCron` continue to produce fatal classification
- All 88 related tests pass across 3 test files

**What was not tested**:

- Full end-to-end cron run with real agent session producing an exec error + recovery (requires live model)
- `run-executor.ts` interim retry path behavior change (analyzed in code: line 602 `isError` hard gate prevents impact)
- Cross-channel behavior (only `announce` delivery mode was analyzed)

### 验证环境

- OS: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64
- Runtime: Node 22.19+
- Branch: `fix/cron-recovered-final-answer-96255`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: A genuinely fatal error without `runLevelError` being set, where `finalAssistantVisibleText` contains only a partial/placeholder message. Mitigation: the `!runLevelError` guard prevents this — provider errors, context overflows, and abort/timeout scenarios all set `runLevelError`.
- **Compatibility impact**: None. This is a pure relaxation of the fatal classification — runs that were previously `error` may become `ok`, but runs that were `ok` remain `ok`. No config, migration, or SDK changes.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios**: exec error without markers + finalAssistantVisibleText (new test), runLevelError precedence (new test), fatalForCron precedence (new test), existing recovery gates unchanged (all 28 existing tests pass)
- **Edge cases checked**: All payloads are errors + finalAssistantVisibleText, earlier non-error payload before trailing error + finalAssistantVisibleText, failureSignal with fatalForCron=true
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live cron run with real agent model producing the exact error pattern from #96255

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Pending
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue #96255 analysis → identified missing recovery gate in `hasFatalStructuredErrorPayload` → designed `hasRecoveredByFinalAnswer` as the minimal addition

Related to #96255
